bank.retain_mission: Extract two kinds of knowledge: (1) Technical facts — decisions, architecture, patterns, conventions, tool configs, solved problems, debugging insights. (2) Behavioral lessons — what the user corrected, what frustrated them, what they explicitly asked the agent to do or stop doing. For corrections and feedback, preserve the user's exact words and emotional intensity — these are the highest-value signals. Ignore transient debugging output, boilerplate, system prompts, and tool call details. Never extract API keys, tokens, passwords, or secrets.
bank.observations_mission: Track two axes: (1) Stable project facts — tech stack, conventions, architecture, tool configs, how codebases evolve. (2) Agent behavior patterns — recurring corrections, developer preferences, mistakes to avoid, workflow rules. When the same correction appears across sessions, escalate it to a strong behavioral rule.

entity_labels.description: Technical domain or area of work

values.description: Dev tools, CLI, build systems, agent skills
A technical decision and its rationale
A developer preference or style choice
A new finding about a tool or system
Something the agent did wrong and must not repeat
A behavioral lesson learned from user feedback

entity_labels.description: Type of user feedback signal (only for feedback items)

mental_models.name: Project Context
mental_models.source_query: What is the project tech stack, architecture, and key conventions? What are the main components and how do they fit together?

mental_models.name: Developer Preferences
mental_models.source_query: What are the developer preferences for tools, libraries, coding style, and workflow? How do they like code to be written and reviewed? What patterns do they insist on?

mental_models.name: Solved Problems & Lessons
mental_models.source_query: What problems have been solved, what debugging approaches worked, and what lessons were learned? Include error messages and their fixes.

mental_models.name: Agent Corrections & Rules
mental_models.source_query: What has the agent done wrong? What corrections has the user given? What behaviors must the agent never repeat? Look for patterns — if the same correction appears multiple times, it is critical.

mental_models.name: Active Work & Open Items
mental_models.source_query: What tasks, features, or issues are currently being worked on or are still unresolved?

directives.name: no-secrets
directives.content: Never store, recall, or reflect API keys, tokens, passwords, connection strings, or other credentials. If a memory contains sensitive data, exclude it.

directives.name: prioritize-corrections
directives.content: User feedback tagged type:correction carries the highest behavioral weight. Emphatic or repeated corrections must surface prominently during recall. A correction that appears across multiple sessions is a pattern — treat it as a hard rule, not a suggestion.

directives.name: focus-on-reusable
directives.content: Prioritize knowledge that will be useful across sessions: architectural decisions, tool configurations, solved problems, and workflow patterns. Deprioritize ephemeral information like specific file contents or transient debug output.
